all_candidates.title: Difficult finish at Stage 15 of Tour de France

transcript.clean_text: Their story really started when Chris Froom came up with a flat tire. Flat tire. But don't forget Sky has been on the front the entire day. So, a lot of tired bodies and not a lot of people left to help Chris once he did have this mechanical. Great work by Michael Kuowski to do a quick wheel change. Not a great push. I would have asked for a little bit of a, you know, get back up to speed, but and then Sergio A now came back. He saw it. He was quick to react. He got halfway across the gap. Ag2R was already on the offensive when that flat happened. So there's no waiting at that point for anybody in the Pelaton. Mikuel Nieve eventually came back. He got him a little bit further across and and Curena but driving at the front. AG2R and then finally that man with his jersey unzip. Mika Landa on the wheel of the Italian champion had to come back after Nbe explodes. Na got Chris Froom pretty close. This is the last category one climb of the day. But all hands on deck for Team Sky. They needed to use every single teammate of Chris Froom to pace him back into the front of the GC contenders. And at the top of the last category one, it was only a few guys left in the front. So a very only one chase. There's only one and that was Mik Lande who luckily was still in that first group. Waited back for his team leader. Pacing back up to the front. Amazing chase by Team Sky and Chris R. The yellow jersey. Had to close about a 40 second gap to get back in that lead group, especially when the the gas was wide open by this man right here and his team A2R and Roman Bard. And guys, the fact that Mikuel Landa came back to get Chris Froom, I back up to a conversation we had before the race yesterday wondering if he was in it for himself had to say a lot about Team Sky that he came back to get Froom. Well, that's pretty emphatic proof that they're racing for Chris Froom. He's in the yellow jersey. And the rest of the tour to France really suits Chris F's talents. The attacks continue to come. This is Simon Yates on the attack. He got some daylight. Vier Moose chasing him down. Still a little bit too much power left in the front group of leaders of the GC men. There's a lot of other groups from the breakaway that are in tatters all the way down the road. Balcomm at this point streaking to the stage went solo off the front and then clear daylight by Dan Martin. Hits him at the right time and was able to hook up with some of the riders that had been dropped from the original break. Pretty surprised to see them cooperate with Dan Martin. I'm not surprised at all. Dan Dan's a good guy. He's been around for a long time and I think that everyone's happy to see Dan doing as good as he really is. Especially after that crash that they had with Richie Port. You have to think if he wouldn't be in that crash, he might even be in that yellow jersey right now. Okay, a lot of could, would Dan's riding fantastic, battling against injury. If anyone saw the video of him trying to get back into his team bus, you see how much pain he is going through, especially when he gets off the bike. Obviously on the bike, he's doing pretty good though. still he said if he could get through with the Pyrenees and this weekend watch out for him in the Alps. Obviously he's coming out back on serious form. We look for big things from Dan up to the line. Chris Froom mad dogging it for really no apparent reason whatsoever sprinting to the line. Yates showing a lot of life here just like his director sports Matty White said earlier. This last third week could be on fire here in the tour to France. See the leaders, the top four in the overall standings coming in together. No movement there. Christian talked about Dan Martin. He's responsible for the only move. He jumps into fifth place. He's now 112 back. Mikuel Landanda falls behind him at six. played a big role in helping Chris Groom remain in yellow.
